# Installing Unity Hub on Linux

Based on the page content provided, this appears to be the Unity Hub documentation site, but the extracted content consists primarily of styling and configuration code rather than installation instructions.

However, I can provide general guidance on Unity Hub installation for Linux:

## General Installation Steps for Linux

1. **Download Unity Hub**
   - Visit the official Unity Hub download page
   - Select the Linux version

2. **Install Dependencies**
   - Ensure you have necessary libraries installed:
   ```bash
   sudo apt-get update
   sudo apt-get install libxss1 libgconf-2-4
   ```

3. **Install Unity Hub**
   - Make the downloaded file executable:
   ```bash
   chmod +x UnityHub.AppImage
   ```
   - Run the installer:
   ```bash
   ./UnityHub.AppImage
   ```

4. **System Requirements**
   - 64-bit Linux OS
   - At least 2GB RAM
   - OpenGL 2.0+ support

## Recommendation

For complete and accurate installation instructions, I recommend:
- Visiting the official [Unity Hub documentation](https://docs.unity3d.com/hub/manual/)
- Checking the [Unity Download page](https://unity.com/download)
- Reviewing platform-specific requirements in the official documentation

The page content you provided appears to be primarily styling and configuration code rather than instructional content, so consulting the official Unity documentation directly would provide more reliable information.

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "Installing Unity on Linux..."

# Update package lists
sudo apt-get update -qq

# Install snapd if not already installed
if ! command -v snap &> /dev/null; then
    echo "Installing snapd..."
    sudo apt-get install -y -qq snapd
    
    # Start snapd service
    sudo systemctl start snapd
    sudo systemctl enable snapd
    
    # Wait for snapd to be ready
    sleep 2
fi

# Install Unity Hub via snap
echo "Installing Unity Hub via snap..."
sudo snap install unity-hub --classic

echo "Unity installation completed successfully."

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "Installing Unity Hub on Linux..."

# Create installation directory
INSTALL_DIR="$HOME/.local/bin"
mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"

# Download Unity Hub AppImage if not already present
APPIMAGE_URL="https://public-cdn.cloud.unity3d.com/hub/prod/UnityHub.AppImage"
APPIMAGE_PATH="$INSTALL_DIR/UnityHub.AppImage"

if [ ! -f "$APPIMAGE_PATH" ]; then
    echo "Downloading Unity Hub AppImage..."
    wget -q "$APPIMAGE_URL" -O "$APPIMAGE_PATH" || {
        echo "Failed to download Unity Hub AppImage"
        exit 1
    }
fi

# Make AppImage executable
chmod +x "$APPIMAGE_PATH"

# Install dependencies for AppImage
echo "Installing required dependencies..."
sudo apt-get install -y -qq libfuse2 libxss1 2>/dev/null || true

# Create symlink in PATH
if [ ! -L "/usr/local/bin/unity-hub" ]; then
    sudo ln -s "$APPIMAGE_PATH" /usr/local/bin/unity-hub
fi

echo "Unity Hub installation completed successfully."
echo "To launch Unity Hub, run: unity-hub"

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "Installing Unity Hub on Linux..."

# Create installation directory
INSTALL_DIR="$HOME/.local/bin"
mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"

# Download Unity Hub AppImage if not already present
APPIMAGE_URL="https://public-cdn.cloud.unity3d.com/hub/prod/UnityHub.AppImage"
APPIMAGE_PATH="$INSTALL_DIR/UnityHub.AppImage"

if [ ! -f "$APPIMAGE_PATH" ]; then
    echo "Downloading Unity Hub AppImage..."
    wget -q "$APPIMAGE_URL" -O "$APPIMAGE_PATH" || {
        echo "Failed to download Unity Hub AppImage"
        exit 1
    }
fi

# Make AppImage executable
chmod +x "$APPIMAGE_PATH"

# Extract AppImage to avoid FUSE requirement
EXTRACT_DIR="$INSTALL_DIR/UnityHub"
if [ ! -d "$EXTRACT_DIR" ]; then
    echo "Extracting Unity Hub AppImage..."
    mkdir -p "$EXTRACT_DIR"
    cd "$EXTRACT_DIR"
    "$APPIMAGE_PATH" --appimage-extract > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
    if [ ! -f "$EXTRACT_DIR/squashfs-root/unityhub" ]; then
        echo "Failed to extract AppImage"
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Create symlink to the extracted binary
BINARY_PATH="$EXTRACT_DIR/squashfs-root/unityhub"
if [ ! -L "/usr/local/bin/unity-hub" ]; then
    sudo ln -sf "$BINARY_PATH" /usr/local/bin/unity-hub
fi

echo "Unity Hub installation completed successfully."
echo "To launch Unity Hub, run: unity-hub"

set -e

if [ ! -z "$DEBUG" ] ; then
  env
  set -x

THIS="$0"
# http://stackoverflow.com/questions/3190818/
args=("$@")

  APPDIR="$path"
fi

#!/bin/bash
# Extract metadata for Unity Hub installation

# Find the binary
BINARY_PATH="/usr/local/bin/unity-hub"
if [ ! -L "$BINARY_PATH" ] && [ ! -f "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    BINARY_PATH="$HOME/.local/bin/UnityHub/squashfs-root/unityhub"
fi

if [ ! -f "$BINARY_PATH" ] && [ ! -L "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    # Try to find it
    BINARY_PATH=$(command -v unity-hub 2>/dev/null || which unityhub-bin 2>/dev/null || echo "")
fi

BINARY_NAME="unity-hub"
DISPLAY_NAME="Unity Hub"
VERSION="3.11.1"

# Find icon paths
ICON_PATHS=()

# Check in extracted app directory
if [ -d "$HOME/.local/bin/UnityHub/squashfs-root/usr/share/icons" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
        ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
    done < <(find "$HOME/.local/bin/UnityHub/squashfs-root/usr/share/icons" -name "unityhub.png" -type f 2>/dev/null)
fi

# Check standard icon locations
for dir in /usr/share/icons ~/.local/share/icons; do
    if [ -d "$dir" ]; then
        while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
        done < <(find "$dir" -name "*unity*" -name "*.png" -o -name "*.svg" 2>/dev/null | head -5)
    fi
done

# Check for desktop entry
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
if [ -f "$HOME/.local/share/applications/Unity Hub.desktop" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="$HOME/.local/share/applications/Unity Hub.desktop"
elif [ -f "$HOME/.local/bin/UnityHub/squashfs-root/unityhub.desktop" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="$HOME/.local/bin/UnityHub/squashfs-root/unityhub.desktop"
fi

# Try to get version from binary
if [ -f "$BINARY_PATH" ] || [ -L "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    ACTUAL_BINARY=$(readlink -f "$BINARY_PATH" 2>/dev/null || echo "$BINARY_PATH")
    if [ -f "$ACTUAL_BINARY" ]; then
        # Get version from the AppImage metadata if available
        APP_DIR=$(dirname "$ACTUAL_BINARY")
        if [ -f "$APP_DIR/../resources/app.asar.unpacked/package.json" ]; then
            VERSION=$(grep -oP '"version":\s*"\K[^"]+' "$APP_DIR/../resources/app.asar.unpacked/package.json" 2>/dev/null || echo "3.11.1")
        fi
    fi
fi

# Build the metadata JSON
ICON_JSON="[]"
if [ ${#ICON_PATHS[@]} -gt 0 ]; then
    ICON_JSON=$(printf '%s\n' "${ICON_PATHS[@]}" | jq -R . | jq -s .)
fi

# Output JSON
cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $([ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ] && echo "\"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\"" || echo "null"),
  "icon_paths": $ICON_JSON,
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "Installing Unity Hub on Linux..."

# Create installation directory
INSTALL_DIR="$HOME/.local/bin"
mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"

# Download Unity Hub AppImage if not already present
APPIMAGE_URL="https://public-cdn.cloud.unity3d.com/hub/prod/UnityHub.AppImage"
APPIMAGE_PATH="$INSTALL_DIR/UnityHub.AppImage"

if [ ! -f "$APPIMAGE_PATH" ]; then
    echo "Downloading Unity Hub AppImage..."
    wget -q "$APPIMAGE_URL" -O "$APPIMAGE_PATH" || {
        echo "Failed to download Unity Hub AppImage"
        exit 1
    }
fi

# Make AppImage executable
chmod +x "$APPIMAGE_PATH"

# Extract AppImage to avoid FUSE requirement
EXTRACT_DIR="$INSTALL_DIR/UnityHub"
if [ ! -d "$EXTRACT_DIR" ]; then
    echo "Extracting Unity Hub AppImage..."
    mkdir -p "$EXTRACT_DIR"
    cd "$EXTRACT_DIR"
    "$APPIMAGE_PATH" --appimage-extract > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
    if [ ! -f "$EXTRACT_DIR/squashfs-root/unityhub" ]; then
        echo "Failed to extract AppImage"
        exit 1
    fi
fi

# Create symlink to the extracted binary
BINARY_PATH="$EXTRACT_DIR/squashfs-root/unityhub"
if [ ! -L "/usr/local/bin/unity-hub" ]; then
    sudo ln -sf "$BINARY_PATH" /usr/local/bin/unity-hub
fi

echo "Unity Hub installation completed successfully."
echo "To launch Unity Hub, run: unity-hub"

#!/bin/bash
# Extract metadata for Unity Hub installation

# Find the binary
BINARY_PATH="/usr/local/bin/unity-hub"
if [ ! -L "$BINARY_PATH" ] && [ ! -f "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    BINARY_PATH="$HOME/.local/bin/UnityHub/squashfs-root/unityhub"
fi

if [ ! -f "$BINARY_PATH" ] && [ ! -L "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    # Try to find it
    BINARY_PATH=$(command -v unity-hub 2>/dev/null || which unityhub-bin 2>/dev/null || echo "")
fi

BINARY_NAME="unity-hub"
DISPLAY_NAME="Unity Hub"
VERSION="3.11.1"

# Find icon paths
ICON_PATHS=()

# Check in extracted app directory
if [ -d "$HOME/.local/bin/UnityHub/squashfs-root/usr/share/icons" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
        ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
    done < <(find "$HOME/.local/bin/UnityHub/squashfs-root/usr/share/icons" -name "unityhub.png" -type f 2>/dev/null)
fi

# Check standard icon locations
for dir in /usr/share/icons ~/.local/share/icons; do
    if [ -d "$dir" ]; then
        while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
        done < <(find "$dir" -name "*unityhub*" \( -name "*.png" -o -name "*.svg" \) 2>/dev/null | head -5)
    fi
done

# Check for desktop entry
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
if [ -f "$HOME/.local/share/applications/Unity Hub.desktop" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="$HOME/.local/share/applications/Unity Hub.desktop"
elif [ -f "$HOME/.local/bin/UnityHub/squashfs-root/unityhub.desktop" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="$HOME/.local/bin/UnityHub/squashfs-root/unityhub.desktop"
fi

# Try to get version from binary
if [ -f "$BINARY_PATH" ] || [ -L "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    ACTUAL_BINARY=$(readlink -f "$BINARY_PATH" 2>/dev/null || echo "$BINARY_PATH")
    if [ -f "$ACTUAL_BINARY" ]; then
        # Get version from the AppImage metadata if available
        APP_DIR=$(dirname "$ACTUAL_BINARY")
        if [ -f "$APP_DIR/../resources/app.asar.unpacked/package.json" ]; then
            VERSION=$(grep -oP '"version":\s*"\K[^"]+' "$APP_DIR/../resources/app.asar.unpacked/package.json" 2>/dev/null || echo "3.11.1")
        fi
    fi
fi

# Build the icon paths JSON array manually
ICON_JSON="["
for i in "${!ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do
    if [ $i -gt 0 ]; then
        ICON_JSON="$ICON_JSON,"
    fi
    ICON_JSON="$ICON_JSON\"${ICON_PATHS[$i]}\""
done
ICON_JSON="$ICON_JSON]"

# Build desktop_entry JSON value
if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY_JSON="\"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\""
else
    DESKTOP_ENTRY_JSON="null"
fi

# Output JSON
cat <<EOF
{
  "binary_path": "$BINARY_PATH",
  "binary_name": "$BINARY_NAME",
  "display_name": "$DISPLAY_NAME",
  "desktop_entry": $DESKTOP_ENTRY_JSON,
  "icon_paths": $ICON_JSON,
  "version": "$VERSION"
}
EOF

    wget -q "$APPIMAGE_URL" -O "$APPIMAGE_PATH" || {
        echo "Failed to download Unity Hub AppImage"
        exit 1
    }

# Make AppImage executable
chmod +x "$APPIMAGE_PATH"

# Extract AppImage to avoid FUSE requirement
EXTRACT_DIR="$INSTALL_DIR/UnityHub"
if [ ! -d "$EXTRACT_DIR" ]; then
    echo "Extracting Unity Hub AppImage..."
    mkdir -p "$EXTRACT_DIR"
    cd "$EXTRACT_DIR"
    "$APPIMAGE_PATH" --appimage-extract > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
    if [ ! -f "$EXTRACT_DIR/squashfs-root/unityhub" ]; then

        exit 1
    }

# Create symlink to the extracted binary
BINARY_PATH="$EXTRACT_DIR/squashfs-root/unityhub"
if [ ! -L "/usr/local/bin/unity-hub" ]; then
    sudo ln -sf "$BINARY_PATH" /usr/local/bin/unity-hub
fi

echo "Unity Hub installation completed successfully."
echo "To launch Unity Hub, run: unity-hub"

#!/bin/bash
# Extract metadata for Unity Hub installation

# Find the binary
BINARY_PATH="/usr/local/bin/unity-hub"
if [ ! -L "$BINARY_PATH" ] && [ ! -f "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    BINARY_PATH="$HOME/.local/bin/UnityHub/squashfs-root/unityhub"
fi

if [ ! -f "$BINARY_PATH" ] && [ ! -L "$BINARY_PATH" ]; then
    # Try to find it
    BINARY_PATH=$(command -v unity-hub 2>/dev/null || which unityhub-bin 2>/dev/null || echo "")
fi

BINARY_NAME=""
DISPLAY_NAME=""
VERSION=""

# Get binary name from the path
if [ -n "$BINARY_PATH" ] && ([ -f "$BINARY_PATH" ] || [ -L "$BINARY_PATH" ]); then
    BINARY_NAME=$(basename "$BINARY_PATH")
fi

# Find and parse desktop entry for display name and version
DESKTOP_ENTRY=""
if [ -f "$HOME/.local/share/applications/Unity Hub.desktop" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="$HOME/.local/share/applications/Unity Hub.desktop"
elif [ -f "$HOME/.local/bin/UnityHub/squashfs-root/unityhub.desktop" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY="$HOME/.local/bin/UnityHub/squashfs-root/unityhub.desktop"
fi

# Extract display name from desktop entry
if [ -f "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DISPLAY_NAME=$(grep -m 1 "^Name=" "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" | sed 's/^Name=//' || echo "")
    # Extract version from desktop entry (X-AppImage-Version key)
    VERSION=$(grep -m 1 "^X-AppImage-Version=" "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" | sed 's/^X-AppImage-Version=//' || echo "")
fi

# Fallback for display name
if [ -z "$DISPLAY_NAME" ]; then
    DISPLAY_NAME="Unity Hub"
fi

# Find icon paths
ICON_PATHS=()

# Check in extracted app directory
if [ -d "$HOME/.local/bin/UnityHub/squashfs-root/usr/share/icons" ]; then
    while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
        ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
    done < <(find "$HOME/.local/bin/UnityHub/squashfs-root/usr/share/icons" -name "unityhub.png" -type f 2>/dev/null)
fi

# Check standard icon locations
for dir in /usr/share/icons ~/.local/share/icons; do
    if [ -d "$dir" ]; then
        while IFS= read -r icon_file; do
            ICON_PATHS+=("$icon_file")
        done < <(find "$dir" -name "*unityhub*" \( -name "*.png" -o -name "*.svg" \) 2>/dev/null | head -5)
    fi
done

# Build the icon paths JSON array manually
ICON_JSON="["
for i in "${!ICON_PATHS[@]}"; do
    if [ $i -gt 0 ]; then
        ICON_JSON="$ICON_JSON,"
    fi
    ICON_JSON="$ICON_JSON\"${ICON_PATHS[$i]}\""
done
ICON_JSON="$ICON_JSON]"

# Build desktop_entry JSON value
if [ -n "$DESKTOP_ENTRY" ]; then
    DESKTOP_ENTRY_JSON="\"$DESKTOP_ENTRY\""
else
    DESKTOP_ENTRY_JSON="null"
fi

# Build version JSON value
if [ -n "$VERSION" ]; then
    VERSION_JSON="\"$VERSION\""
else
    VERSION_JSON="null"
fi

# Output JSON
cat <<EOF
{
  \"binary_path\": \"$BINARY_PATH\",
  \"binary_name\": \"$BINARY_NAME\",
  \"display_name\": \"$DISPLAY_NAME\",

  \"icon_paths\": $ICON_JSON,
  \"version\": $VERSION_JSON
}

#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "Installing Unity Hub on Linux..."

# Create installation directory
INSTALL_DIR="$HOME/.local/bin"
mkdir -p "$INSTALL_DIR"

# Download Unity Hub AppImage if not already present
APPIMAGE_URL="https://public-cdn.cloud.unity3d.com/hub/prod/UnityHub.AppImage"
APPIMAGE_PATH="$INSTALL_DIR/UnityHub.AppImage"

if [ ! -f "$APPIMAGE_PATH" ]; then
    echo "Downloading Unity Hub AppImage..."
    wget -q "$APPIMAGE_URL" -O "$APPIMAGE_PATH" || {
        echo "Failed to download Unity Hub AppImage"
        exit 1
    }
fi

# Make AppImage executable
chmod +x "$APPIMAGE_PATH"

# Extract AppImage to avoid FUSE requirement
EXTRACT_DIR="$INSTALL_DIR/UnityHub"
if [ ! -d "$EXTRACT_DIR" ]; then
    echo "Extracting Unity Hub AppImage..."
    mkdir -p "$EXTRACT_DIR"
    cd "$EXTRACT_DIR"
    "$APPIMAGE_PATH" --appimage-extract > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
    if [ ! -f "$EXTRACT_DIR/squashfs-root/unityhub" ]; then
        echo "Failed to extract AppImage"
        exit 1
    }
fi

# Create symlink to the extracted binary
BINARY_PATH="$EXTRACT_DIR/squashfs-root/unityhub"
if [ ! -L "/usr/local/bin/unity-hub" ]; then
    sudo ln -sf "$BINARY_PATH" /usr/local/bin/unity-hub
fi

echo "Unity Hub installation completed successfully."
echo "To launch Unity Hub, run: unity-hub"

set -e
